answers.field_label: What Would You Like To Address
answers.value: For the past few months I’ve had intermittent sensitivity and a dull ache on the upper left side when chewing harder foods (like nuts or crusty bread), and occasional sharp sensitivity to cold drinks on a few lower teeth. I’m also concerned that my gums look a little red and puffy in some areas and I’ve noticed small amounts of bleeding when I floss, especially around my back molars. In addition, I have an old silver filling on a lower right molar that feels rough with my tongue and sometimes catches food; I’m wondering if it needs to be replaced, possibly with a tooth-colored filling. I’d also like an overall evaluation of my bite—my jaw sometimes feels a bit tight in the mornings, and my wife says she hears me grind my teeth at night. Finally, I’m interested in talking about whitening options, but only after any necessary treatment is done, because I drink coffee and my teeth have gotten a bit yellow over the years.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Any Previous Major Dental Treatment Why
answers.value: Root canal and crown on upper right first molar (tooth #3) after a deep cavity and recurrent infection. Treatment was done over a couple of visits with Dr. Whitfield, including endodontic therapy and placement of a porcelain-fused-to-metal crown. No major complications, but the tooth was very sensitive for a few weeks afterward.

answers.field_label: Is There Anything You Would Like To Add Line 1
answers.value: I tend to get anxious before dental appointments because of a bad experience I had as a teenager when a filling was done without enough numbing, so I really appreciate when the dentist explains what’s happening step by step and checks in frequently about pain or discomfort. I usually respond well to local anesthesia, but it can take a little longer to kick in on my lower right side, so in the past they’ve sometimes had to give an extra carpule for that area. I don’t have any known medication allergies, but I do get a bit lightheaded if I haven’t eaten, so I try to schedule morning appointments after a light breakfast.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Is There Anything You Would Like To Add Line 2
answers.value: I work full time as a project manager and have two young kids, so flexibility with early-morning or late-afternoon appointments is very helpful for me. I’m motivated to get my mouth in good shape and keep it that way—I’d appreciate clear priorities if there are multiple issues (for example, what needs to be done right away vs. what can wait a few months). I’m also interested in learning specifically what I can adjust in my home care (flossing technique, use of interdental brushes, night guard for grinding, etc.) to reduce sensitivity and prevent future problems, rather than only reacting when something hurts.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
